subject: Re: M Masks
body:
Hi 3P1L,

M expects a binary mask and does the extension/smoothing itself for each 
iteration based on current resolution. However, using a non-binary mask 
shouldn't cause a crash. Masks from Relion usually import without problems. 
Can you share the mask file you're trying to use?

I don't understand the "import masks from Warp" part. Warp can only 
generate 2D masks for individual micrographs during BoxNet picking.

Cheers,
2I1Y


On Tuesday, August 11, 2020 at 3:41:38 PM UTC+2, 3P1L wrote:
>
> *Dear All,*
>
> I have a question regarding masks in M. Whenever I try to import masks 
> from Warp, they fail to display properly and unfortunately result in a 
> crash during refinement.
>
> Using the built-in function to create a mask (filter to X Å and use 
> threshold of Y), I'm a bit afraid that the mask will not be 100% correct 
> and 'too sharp'
>
> For example, I cannot extend by a few pixels and set a smooth falloff on 
> the borders. I think that might be responsible for some crashes, too.
>
> Does anybody have a solution for this? Are there any special requirements 
> for masks in M, or do you know how to properly import Relion masks?
>
>
> Thanks for any advice!
>
>
> Phil.
>
